title: CBN postpones MPC meeting
description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has postponed the Monetary Policy Committee’s 293rd meeting, which was scheduled for September 25 and 26, 2023. A statement by the apex  bank’s Director of Corporate
html:
The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has postponed the Monetary Policy Committee’s 293rd meeting, which was scheduled for September 25 and 26, 2023.

A statement by the apex  bank’s Director of Corporate Communications, Dr Isa AbdulMumin, said on Thursday.

The statement read, “The Monetary Policy Committee of the Central Bank of Nigeria has deferred its 293rd meeting scheduled for Monday and Tuesday, September 25 and 26, 2023, respectively.

“A new date will be communicated in due course. We regret any inconvenience this change may cause our stakeholders and the general public.”

Although the apex bank did not provide any reason for the postponement, some operators said it was connected to the vacancy created as a result of suspended CBN governor and subsequent removal of four deputy governors.

However, the newly appointed governor and deputy governors of the bank are yet to resume due to the senate confirmation they awaites.

Last week, President Bola Tinubu nominated Yemi Cardoso to head the CBN.  He also approved the nomination of four new deputy governors to serve for a similar duration of five years in the first instance.

The nominees are Emem Usoro, Muhammad Abdullahi-Dattijo, Philip Ikeazor, and Bala Bello.
